audience_description: Cambridge Exam Officers (legacy field name: cambridge_coordinator) in Eduversal partner schools that hold Cambridge Approved Centre status. Owns: Cambridge candidate entries, exam logistics, internal moderation, syllabus updates, examiner-report-driven scheme-of-work adjustments, and the Centre's compliance with Cambridge International Handbook for Centres. The most error-intolerant role in the school — wrong entries mean students sit wrong papers.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge International Handbook for Centres (current edition)
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Defines Centre obligations: candidate entries, exam administration, internal moderation, syllabus delivery, malpractice protocols. Cambridge Exam Officer is Centre's primary point of accountability.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge Malpractice Regulations
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Defines what constitutes malpractice and the Centre's reporting obligations. Severe consequences for breach including loss of Approved Centre status.

regulatory_anchors.title: Cambridge Centre Agreement (signed by yayasan + Cambridge)
regulatory_anchors.relevance: Contractual obligations specific to this Centre.

framework_anchors.cambridge_school_leader_standards_2023: Anchored in instructional leadership + operations & management dimensions.

priority_levels.compliance: Cambridge-required. Centre status depends on this. Non-negotiable.

recurring_tasks.title: Monitor Cambridge Centre announcements and bulletins
recurring_tasks.description: Read all Cambridge International circulars, syllabus update notices, and Centre bulletins this week. Anything material — propagate to Subject Leaders within 48h.
recurring_tasks.first_time_guide: Cambridge updates can change syllabus content, marking schemes, or paper structure mid-year. A Centre that misses a bulletin and prepares students for the wrong format is a Cambridge complaint waiting to happen. Daily inbox check, weekly summary.

recurring_tasks.title: Weekly check-in with each Cambridge Subject Leader
recurring_tasks.description: 15-minute touch-base with each Subject Leader holding a Cambridge subject. Topics: candidate progress, syllabus pacing, mock results, any Cambridge correspondence affecting them.

recurring_tasks.title: Maintain Centre compliance file currency
recurring_tasks.description: Keep the Cambridge Centre compliance file current: certifications, staff lists, room allocations, exam invigilation rota, malpractice protocols, candidate identification process.

monthly_tasks.title: Monthly candidate entry audit
monthly_tasks.description: Cross-check current candidate entries against student progress data. Flag any candidate whose progress suggests entry adjustment (paper tier change, option swap). Surface to Subject Leader for decision.

termly_tasks.title: Termly syllabus + examiner report review
termly_tasks.description: Within 4 weeks of Cambridge results release: cross-department review of examiner reports, identification of required scheme-of-work adjustments, dissemination to Subject Leaders.

tasks.title: Annual Cambridge Centre compliance review
tasks.description: Complete review of Centre status: approved syllabuses, staff Cambridge IDs, inspection date, any conditions on Centre Agreement. File annual compliance certification with Principal.

tasks.title: Distribute the year's Cambridge calendar to all relevant staff
tasks.description: All entry deadlines, exam windows, results release dates for the year. Confirmed in writing to Subject Leaders, Principal, Academic Coordinator.

tasks.title: Initiate Cambridge Oct/Nov entry collection
tasks.description: Distribute candidate entry forms to Subject Leaders. Collect signed candidate lists. Prepare entries for submission.

8.phase: Late August — Cambridge Oct/Nov standard entry deadline

tasks.title: Submit Cambridge Oct/Nov entries — final deadline
tasks.description: Submit all confirmed candidate entries via the Cambridge Direct portal. Cross-check with Subject Leader sign-off + Principal sign-off. NO changes accepted by Cambridge after standard deadline without late fee.
tasks.first_time_guide: Triple-check candidate names, dates of birth, paper codes, options, tiers. A typo here is not a typo — it is a candidate sitting the wrong paper. Compare every line against Subject Leader sign-off before clicking submit.

tasks.title: Late entry window — final adjustments
tasks.description: Last opportunity to add candidates or adjust entries (with late fee). After this, no changes possible.

tasks.title: Oct/Nov exam administration
tasks.description: Daily exam administration: invigilation, paper security, candidate identification, special considerations management, malpractice protocol enforcement.

tasks.title: Cambridge March/Feb (alternative cycle) registration
tasks.description: If Centre offers March/Feb cycle for AS/A-Level retake candidates: collect entries, submit via Direct portal.

tasks.title: Coordinate Oct/Nov results release
tasks.description: Receive results from Cambridge. Distribute candidate statements securely. Coordinate Subject Leader cross-analysis. Prepare results report for yayasan and Eduversal HQ.

tasks.title: Post-results enquiry / re-mark coordination
tasks.description: Within 2 weeks of results: coordinate any candidate or Subject Leader requests for enquiry-about-results or re-marks. Submit to Cambridge within deadline.

25.phase: Late February — May/June standard entry deadline

tasks.title: Submit Cambridge May/June entries — standard deadline
tasks.description: Same protocol as Oct/Nov — entry forms, sign-offs, submission via Direct portal. May/June is the larger cycle for most subjects — extra rigour required.

tasks.title: May/June late entry adjustments — final window
tasks.description: Last opportunity for additions or adjustments to May/June entries.

tasks.title: May/June exam administration — peak operations
tasks.description: Daily exam administration through the longest series of the year. Coursework submission deadlines. Special considerations cases. Malpractice protocols. Daily log essential.
tasks.first_time_guide: This is the role's hardest period. Plan annual leave around it, not into it. Identify a backup deputy in W30 — if you fall ill, the Centre still operates.

provenance.regulatory_sources: Cambridge Handbook for Centres (current edition), Cambridge Malpractice Regulations, Cambridge Centre Agreement (per-school)
provenance.framework_sources: Cambridge School Leader Standards 2023, Eduversal Leadership Competency Framework
provenance.operational_pattern_source: Eduversal-network specific role definition. The Cambridge Coordinator function is sub-clinical at most partner schools; this JSON formalises the role expectations in the same governance rhythm pattern as the rest of the network.

naming_clarification: The platform key 'cambridge_coordinator' is the existing ah_sub_role value in the codebase (`ah_sub_roles[]` includes 'cambridge_coordinator'). The user-facing label is 'Cambridge Exam Officer' which is the operational title in most partner schools. The schema retains the platform key for backward compatibility with weekly_templates / weekly_progress doc-id construction; UI labels can show 'Cambridge Exam Officer' freely.
